follow-up.chief_complaint: Follow-up on hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus
follow-up.history_of_present_illness: 58-year-old male presents for follow-up of hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus. Reports good adherence to lisinopril 20 mg daily and metformin 500 mg BID. Home BP readings averaging 130s/80s. Denies chest pain, shortness of breath, and dizziness. Reports occasional tingling in feet, worse at night. Most recent A1c from last month is 7.1, down from 7.8.
plan: Continue current medications (lisinopril 20 mg daily and metformin 500 mg BID)
Patient to schedule lipid panel within 2 weeks
Return to clinic in 3 months, or sooner if foot symptoms worsen

follow_ups.timeframe: 3 months, or sooner if foot symptoms worsen

red_flags: Occasional tingling in feet, worse at night (possible early peripheral neuropathy)
extraction_notes: Route of administration not specified for either medication; assumed oral based on context but not explicitly stated
Temperature, respiratory rate, and oxygen saturation not documented in the visit note

new-patient.history_of_present_illness: 34-year-old female presenting with bilateral, pressure-like headaches occurring 3–4 times per week, worse in late afternoon. Denies aura, vision changes, or nausea/vomiting. Works remotely with high screen time and reports poor sleep (5–6 hours).
assessment: Likely lifestyle contributors (screen time, poor sleep)
plan: Trial of OTC ibuprofen 400 mg PRN, max 3×/week

follow_ups.action: Return visit or sooner if red flag symptoms develop

extraction_notes: Respiratory rate not documented in note
Patient given education on red flag symptoms (worst headache of life, focal deficits, fever with stiff neck) and advised to return sooner if these occur
Sulfa allergy reaction details unspecified per patient; documented as 'unknown reaction'
No current medications listed; ibuprofen is being trialed as new OTC recommendation, not currently prescribed

urgent-care.chief_complaint: Right ankle pain after inversion injury playing basketball
urgent-care.history_of_present_illness: 22-year-old male presents with right ankle pain sustained approximately 3 hours ago during a basketball game from an inversion injury. Patient is able to bear weight for 4 steps with pain. Physical examination reveals swelling over the lateral malleolus and tenderness to palpation in the anterior talofibular region, with no bony tenderness at the posterior malleolus or base of the 5th metatarsal. Ottawa criteria are negative.
plan: Crutches as needed for first 48 hours
Ibuprofen 600 mg three times daily with food for 5 days

follow_ups.action: Follow up with PCP if not improving

extraction_notes: No respiratory rate or oxygen saturation documented in the note
No weight documented in the note
No drug allergies documented in the note
Return precautions listed are patient counseling points rather than clinical red flags identified at time of visit
